review.summary: Code Beat found 1 actionable finding(s): 1 major. Score 2/5 reflects the highest-severity issue raised by the reviewer agents.

findings.title: applyDiscount missing input validation for out-of-range percent values
findings.body:
The `applyDiscount` function performs no bounds checking on the `percent` parameter. This produces semantically incorrect results for two classes of inputs:

1. **Negative percent**: `applyDiscount(100, -10)` returns 110 — the price increases instead of being discounted.
2. **Percent > 100**: `applyDiscount(100, 200)` returns -100 — a negative final price.

The existing `src/pricing.ts` (line 11–20) already demonstrates the correct pattern: it returns `subtotal` unchanged when `discountPercent <= 0` and returns `0` when `discountPercent >= 100`. The new function should adopt similar guards to avoid surprising behavior for callers who pass out-of-range values.
